Package recoder.java

Interface NonTerminalProgramElement

All Superinterfaces:
ModelElement, ProgramElement, SourceElement
All Known Subinterfaces:
ConstructorReference, Declaration, ExpressionContainer, MemberDeclaration, MemberReference, NamedProgramElement, NameReference, PackageReferenceContainer, ParameterContainer, ReferenceSuffix, ScopeDefiningElement, StatementContainer, TypeDeclarationContainer, TypeReferenceContainer, TypeReferenceInfix, TypeScope, VariableScope

RKeYMetaConstruct, RKeYMetaConstructExpression, RKeYMetaConstructType, RMethodBodyStatement, RMethodCallStatement, SeqConcat, SeqGet, SeqIndexOf, SeqLength, SeqPut, SeqReverse, SeqSingleton, SeqSub, SuperConstructorReference, SuperReference, Switch, SynchronizedBlock, Then, ThisConstructorReference, ThisReference, Throw, Throws, Times, TimesAssignment, TransactionStatement, Try, TypeArgumentDeclaration, TypeCast, TypeDeclaration, TypeOperator, TypeParameterDeclaration, TypeReference, TypeSVWrapper, UncollatedReferenceQualifier, UnsignedShiftRight, UnsignedShiftRightAssignment, VariableDeclaration, VariableReference, VariableSpecification, While
public interface NonTerminalProgramElement extends ProgramElement
Non terminal program element.

    validate

    getASTParent, getComments, setComments

  • Method Details

    • getChildCount

      int getChildCount()
      Returns the number of children of this node.
      Returns:
      an int giving the number of children of this node

    • getChildAt

      ProgramElement getChildAt(int index)
      Returns the child at the specified index in this node's "virtual" child array.
      Parameters:
      index - an index into this node's "virtual" child array
      Returns:
      the program element at the given position
      Throws:
      ArrayIndexOutOfBoundsException - if index is out of bounds

    • getIndexOfChild

      int getIndexOfChild(ProgramElement child)
      Returns the index of the given child, or -1 if there is no such child. The child is searched for by identity: getChildAt(getIndexOfChild(x)) == x.
      Parameters:
      child - the exact child to look for.
      Returns:
      the index of the given child, or -1.

    • getChildPositionCode

      int getChildPositionCode(ProgramElement child)
      Returns the positional code of the given child, or -1 if there is no such child. The result contains an encoding of the relative position of the child as well as the role it has been playing in this parent element. This information is required internally for proper undo of transformations and is to be delivered to the detached method of the ChangeHistory.
      Parameters:
      child - the exact child to look for.
      Returns:
      the positional code of the given child, or -1.
      See Also:
      • ChangeHistory.detached(recoder.java.ProgramElement, recoder.java.NonTerminalProgramElement, int)

    • getIndexOfChild

      @Deprecated int getIndexOfChild(int positionCode)
      Deprecated.
      Extracts the index of a child from its position code.

      This method does not return the child index as received by getIndexOfChild(), but rather the index within internal data structure representation.

      Therefore it is common that getIndexOfChild(getChildPositionCode(aChild)) != getIndexOfChild(aChild)

      This method is deprecated as of 0.75

      Parameters:
      positionCode - the position code.
      Returns:
      the index of the given position code.
      See Also:
      • getChildPositionCode(ProgramElement)

    • getRoleOfChild

      int getRoleOfChild(int positionCode)
      Extracts the role of a child from its position code. This information is required internally for proper undo of transformations.
      Parameters:
      positionCode - the position code.
      Returns:
      the role code of the given position code.
      See Also:
      • getChildPositionCode(ProgramElement)

    • makeParentRoleValid

      void makeParentRoleValid()
      Ensures that each child has "this" as syntactical parent. Any class should define this method for added attributes, and delegate to super.makeParentRoleValid() to ensure that inherited attributes are made valid as well (e.g. comments from SourceElement). Any constructor of a concrete class should call this method before returning if it shall leave a consistent state.

    • makeAllParentRolesValid

      void makeAllParentRolesValid()
      Calls makeParentRoleValid()for each non terminal in the subtree with the current element as root. If this instanceof TerminalProgramElement, nothing happens.

    • validateAll

      void validateAll() throws ModelException
      Calls ModelElement.validate()for each for the entire subtree with the current element as root.
      Throws:
      ModelException
      Since:
      0.80

    • replaceChild

      boolean replaceChild(ProgramElement p, ProgramElement q)
      Replace a single non-null child in the current node. The child to replace is matched by identity and hence must be known exactly. The replacement element can be null - in that case, the child is effectively removed. The parent role of the new child is validated, while the parent link of the replaced child is left untouched.
      Parameters:
      p - the old child.
      q - the new child.
      Returns:
      true if a replacement has occured, false otherwise.
      Throws:
      ClassCastException - if the new child cannot take over the role of the old one.

    • compilationUnit

      default CompilationUnit compilationUnit()
      Get the compilation unit that contains this program element.
      Returns:
      the compilation unit
